codeReview.summary: The feature adds the unlock screen and a broad Jest suite, but the lockout path is wired against the wrong error codes. In production, `unlockAgent()` rethrows `VAULT_ERROR` for native lockout failures, so this screen falls back to the generic error path instead of showing the required clear lockout message.

issues.description: The lockout branch only matches raw `BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT` / `BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT_PERMANENT` plus a non-existent `VAULT_ERROR_BIOMETRY_LOCKOUT` code (`biometric-unlock-screen.tsx:35-38,203-204`). The real unlock flow goes through `BiometricVault.mapNativeErrorToVaultError()`, which normalizes both native lockout codes to `VAULT_ERROR` (`src/lib/enbox/biometric-vault.ts:433-437`). As a result, an actual OS lockout from `useAgentStore.unlockAgent()` will render the generic error message instead of the required clear lockout UX. The tests miss this because they only mock raw lockout codes / repeated `AUTH_FAILED` (`biometric-unlock-screen.test.tsx:207-245`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, but the transcript skeleton does not show the required `.factory/library/architecture.md` or `.factory/library/user-testing.md` reads before implementation, even though this feature touches the vault/store boundary and relies on the `Unlock with` UI anchor.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `mobile-feature-worker/SKILL.md:20-22,28` requires reading `architecture.md` for vault/store work and `user-testing.md` for user-facing anchors. The transcript skeleton lists reads for `mission.md`, `validation-contract.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.factory/services.yaml`, screen files, `session-store.ts`, `agent-store.ts`, etc., but no read of those library files before coding; the handoff still reports `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Review failed. The screen/test addition is substantial, but real lockout errors from the vault are normalized to `VAULT_ERROR`, so the implemented screen does not satisfy the required lockout UX on the production unlock path.
